game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Tone Sphere:** Tone Sphere is a 3D rhythm game where players interact with musical notes that emerge from a dynamic, spherical environment and travel towards the center of the screen. Players must precisely tap, hold, or swipe on the screen as these notes align with a central target zone, with different note types requiring specific touch inputs (e.g., a single tap for circular notes, a sustained press for long notes, or a directional swipe for arrow notes). Accuracy is graded (e.g., Perfect, Great, Good, Miss), contributing to a score and combo multiplier, while a health bar (e.g., 100 HP) depletes on misses and recovers on accurate inputs; the song ends if the health bar reaches zero. Progression involves completing any of the 80+ songs across 250+ levels to unlock new tracks and higher difficulty settings on an interconnected "sphere map," with difficulty scaling through increased note density, faster approach speeds, and more complex multi-finger patterns. The primary objective is to achieve the highest possible score and maintain a long combo chain through each song.
game_info.description: Tone Sphere is a rhythm game where musical notes emerge from the edges of the screen and travel towards a central target zone. Time your key presses perfectly as notes reach the center to score points and build combos. Different colored notes require different keys. Miss too many notes and your health will deplete, ending the song early. Complete songs to unlock new tracks and increase difficulty for higher scores!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (←↑→↓): Hit corresponding colored directional notes
Space: Hit white circular notes
Z: Hit blue square notes
Shift: Hit special orange triangle notes
Enter: Start game from menu
ESC: Pause/unpause during gameplay
R: Return to start screen from game over

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and interaction mechanics by randomly pressing valid gameplay keys. Validates that the game accepts inputs, processes note hits/misses, and updates score accordingly. This is a baseline test to ensure fundamental game mechanics work.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Randomly select from available input keys (arrows, space, Z, shift) with weighted probability. Higher weight on more common note types. Introduce small random delays between inputs to simulate human-like timing variance.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Game should run without errors. Score should increase on successful hits. Health should decrease on misses. Game should eventually reach GAME_OVER state (either win by completing song or lose by depleting health). No crashes or freezes.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests optimal gameplay strategy to win the game. Implements perfect timing algorithm that calculates exact moment to hit each note as it reaches the center target zone. Validates win condition achievement.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze all active notes and their positions. Calculate distance from center for each note. When a note is within the "perfect" hit window (very close to center), press the corresponding key. Prioritize notes by proximity to maximize accuracy and combo.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Should achieve high accuracy rate (>90% perfect/great hits). Should maintain long combo chains. Should complete the song successfully with high score. Game should reach GAME_OVER_WIN state. Health should remain above zero throughout.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests edge cases and resilience by intentionally playing poorly at first, then recovering. Validates that health depletion mechanics work correctly and that players can recover from mistakes.
TEST_3.strategy_description: For the first 30% of notes, deliberately miss or hit with poor timing. Then switch to optimal strategy for remaining notes. Tests health recovery mechanics and validates that the game properly tracks health changes.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Health should decrease significantly during poor performance phase. Should recover partially during good performance phase. Game should either reach GAME_OVER_LOSE if health depletes to zero, or GAME_OVER_WIN if health sustains. Validates health system works correctly.
